data.narrative:
eleQtron is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised €50M+ to date under founder Jan Leisse. The valuation assessment draws on a universe of 11 comparable companies across quantum computing: 3 public firms (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum via tickers IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Traditional peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing peer set lacks sufficient comparable companies at the same stage or adjacent stage with similar valuation tier characteristics. Of the 7 same-sector peers identified, none of the narrow filters—matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded three or more datapoints necessary for reliable median calculation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in business models, development stage, and capital intensity across quantum computing subsegments.

The absence of robust comparable transaction or revenue-multiple data limits quantitative triangulation at this time. A credible valuation would require either expansion of the peer set through broader stage inclusion, bottom-up revenue or cost-of-capital modeling, or detailed reference pricing from recent Series A transactions in the sector. Without these inputs, no specific valuation range can be supported by the available data.
